_risk_level_doc: low — no new code changes required; this is a duplicate dispatch verification. SMALL tier and low risk_level → test_writer_expected = false.

requirement.original: Integrate the sentinel grant check into hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh so that Write-overwrite operations can be authorized via the sentinel-grant mechanism already implemented in hooks/lib/allowlist.py. The fix: before the existing-file block in pretool-write-guard.sh, call the allowlist sentinel checker. If a valid matching grant exists, allow the write (and let posttool-allowlist-consume.py consume the grant on terminal result).
requirement.clarified: ALREADY IMPLEMENTED (main-agent scope). This is a duplicate dispatch of cycle 20260522-080646-B. All three target files were modified in commit becffc6 and QA passed at iter3 (qa-report-20260522-080646-B-iter3.json). One residual gap: subagent Write-overwrites are not covered by the sentinel (inside _WG_IS_SUB guard).
requirement.what: Verify existing sentinel-grant integration in Write-guard hook and posttool consume; note subagent-scope gap
requirement.why: Duplicate orchestrator dispatch — prior cycle 20260522-080646-B already implemented this. Dev must confirm no re-implementation is needed.

included: Verification that implementation is present at HEAD e109534c
Documentation of subagent-scope gap for orchestrator awareness
excluded: Subagent Write-sentinel extension (new requirement — requires new cycle)

success_criteria: Dev confirms no file modifications needed (status: already_implemented)
Dev documents that main-agent sentinel path works and subagent path is out of scope
AC1-AC4 pass without any code changes
constraints: Must NOT re-implement what is already in HEAD
If subagent scope is desired, must be a separate cycle
Dirty worktree: posttool-allowlist-consume.py has unrelated changes — do not mix into this cycle

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Sentinel check for Write is main-agent-only (inside _WG_IS_SUB != '1' guard); subagent Write-overwrites with matching sentinel still blocked. Codex live probe confirmed rc=2 for subagent input with matching sentinel. Original AC2 scoped is_subagent=false. Extension to subagents requires new cycle.

Dirty worktree version differs from HEAD at Write branch (HEAD line 158 vs worktree line 222); unrelated _finalize_deferred_commit_grant function added to dirty worktree. Line citations in downstream artifacts must use HEAD not worktree numbers.

component_chain.role: PreToolUse hook: calls match_sentinel_grant_for_write via inline Python; exits 0 on match (main-agent only, inside _WG_IS_SUB != '1' guard)

component_chain.role: PostToolUse hook: consumes Write sentinel on any terminal result via 3-candidate task_id lookup + consume_sentinel_grant_on_terminal_result()

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed BA's core duplicate finding is correct for main-agent scope. Raised three issues: (1) subagent scope not covered — sentinel check is inside _WG_IS_SUB guard, subagent Write with matching sentinel returns rc=2 (live probe confirmed); (2) line citation error — posttool Write branch is at HEAD line 158, not worktree line 222; (3) grant creation path (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:98-103, 226-229) not cited in BA evidence. All three applied in the spec/context.

findings.issue: Scope may be incomplete: current implementation only honors Write sentinels for main-agent calls. Subagent Write-overwrites with matching sentinel still blocked (rc=2).
findings.proposed_fix: Either narrow BA conclusion to 'already implemented for main-agent Write-overwrites only; prior AC2 explicitly scoped is_subagent=false,' or implement full-scope support by hoisting sentinel check outside _WG_IS_SUB guard. BA chose: narrow conclusion and log subagent gap as out_of_scope_observation.
findings.rationale: The original requirement background and original AC2 explicitly scoped is_subagent=false. The subagent extension is a NEW requirement and should be a separate cycle. BA conclusion narrowed accordingly.

findings.issue: Provenance/citation error: BA mixed HEAD and dirty worktree line numbers. posttool Write consume branch is at HEAD line 158, not worktree line 222-272.
findings.proposed_fix: Cite HEAD:e109534 lines 158-208 for posttool Write branch.
findings.rationale: Corrected in spec and context JSON — all posttool citations now use HEAD line 158.

findings.issue: Duplicate conclusion should prove the grant can actually be created. userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:98-103 and 226-229 handle /allow Write /path sentinel creation.
findings.proposed_fix: Add evidence note citing those lines so the 'already implemented' conclusion covers the full flow: grant creation → pretool authorization → posttool consumption.
findings.rationale: Added grant-writer as component_chain entry and cited userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:98-103, 226-229 in Evidence section.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All three Codex findings applied: (1) BA conclusion narrowed to main-agent scope with subagent gap logged as out_of_scope_observation; (2) posttool line citations corrected to HEAD line 158; (3) grant-writer path (userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh) cited in evidence and component_chain.
